question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: NetworkOptions()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by exploiting the fact that operationTypes includes types.OperationTypeFee appended after GetStringValuesFromInt32StringMap(), and if a client uses this list to filter transactions, the presence of the fee type may cause it to misclassify transactions and diverge from the real network state?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by sending requests that cause the DB to return a very large number of peers (e.g., thousands of address book entries), causing the NetworkStatusResponse to be so large that it exceeds HTTP response size limits and causes clients to fail to parse the peer list?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by exploiting the fact that the service does not validate that the Peers list returned by addressBookEntryRepo.Entries() contains at least one entry before returning a NetworkStatusResponse, causing clients to receive a valid response with no peers and believe the network is unreachable?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by sending requests that cause the DB to return peers with IPv6 addresses in the ip_address_v4 column (due to a DB misconfiguration), causing clients that only support IPv4 to fail to connect to any network node?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by exploiting the fact that the service does not validate the port values in endpoint strings returned by the DB, causing clients to receive endpoints with invalid port numbers (e.g., 0 or 65536) that prevent them from connecting to network nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by sending requests that cause the DB to return a peer list where all nodes have the same account ID (due to a DB join issue with coalesce(n.account_id, abe.node_account_id)), causing clients to route all transactions to a single node and effectively partition the rest of the network?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by exploiting the fact that the service does not implement any circuit breaker for repeated DB failures in RetrieveGenesis(), RetrieveLatest(), or Entries(), causing the service to continue accepting requests and returning errors indefinitely, amplifying the partition effect?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by sending requests that cause the DB to return a genesis block with a future timestamp (greater than the current block timestamp), causing clients to compute a negative sync progress and believe the network is in an invalid state?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by exploiting the fact that the service does not validate that CurrentBlockIdentifier.Hash is a valid hex string before returning it, causing clients that perform hash validation to reject the response and consider the network partitioned?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by sending requests that cause the DB to return a block with a zero or negative index in RetrieveLatest(), causing clients to reject the block identifier as invalid and refuse to sync with the network?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by exploiting the fact that the service does not validate the consistency between the network identifier in the incoming NetworkRequest and n.network, allowing clients to query status for a different network and receive responses that cause them to diverge from the real network?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by sending requests that cause the DB to return a very old genesis block timestamp (e.g., Unix epoch 0), causing clients to compute an extremely large chain age and potentially overflow timestamp arithmetic, leading to incorrect sync decisions?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by exploiting the fact that the service does not implement any backpressure mechanism, allowing an attacker to send requests faster than the DB can respond, causing the service to queue up requests indefinitely and exhaust memory, making it unavailable to all clients?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by sending requests that cause the DB to return peers with empty account ID strings (when EntityId.String() returns an empty string), causing clients to receive peers with no account ID and fail to route transactions to those nodes?
